summary: Excellent audience recording of a warm, dark ’73 show featuring a stellar Weather Report Suite and a mesmerizing Wharf Rat > Mississippi Half‑Step jam.
review: Fans rave about this 1973 performance as one of the year’s best – a mellow first set highlighted by Keith’s piano on “Ramble On Rose” and “Big River,” a flawless Weather Report Suite, and a wildly inventive second set with the legendary Wharf Rat > Mississippi Half‑Step leading into an unforgettable “Playin'” jam and a sweet Dew encore.  The audience tape captures an oddly ambient but surprisingly clear soundstage that lets listeners hear the interplay between Keith and Bob, making the recording itself a joy to hear.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high‑star reviews (mostly 5★) praise both the musical performance and the surprising clarity of the audience recording; only one low‑star review criticizes release packaging, not the show.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience tape noted for its ambient vibe that adds to the mood, with unusually good instrument separation (Keith vs. Bob) and clear enough detail to enjoy the jam sections.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A warm, mellow first set that builds to a superb Weather Report close; the second set takes an adventurous turn with the odd‑but‑effective Wharf Rat > Half‑Step segue into a soaring Playin' jam and ends with a sweet Dew encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo on the “Can’t You See”/Mind Left Body jam is highlighted; occasional references to his lead work throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as ‘battling giant dinosaurs’ in the second‑set jam and praised for vocal harmonies and backing vocals later in the set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Interplay with Keith noted as a highlight of the mix; praised for strong contributions during Weather Report and other jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano work is repeatedly lauded, especially on “Ramble On Rose,” “Big River,” and throughout the second‑set improvisations.
